zKThis module implements various useful common functions for shrinking tasks.c
d d!
d"d#
Shrinkerz
A Shrinker object manages a single value and a predicate it should
    satisfy, and attempts to improve it in some direction, making it smaller
    and simpler.FNc

Shrinker.__repr__c
Runs initial setup code.
        Convenience function for children that doesn't require messing
        with the signature of init.
        Nr
Shrinker.setupc
z~Delegates shrinking to another shrinker class, by converting the
        current value to and from it with provided functions.c

Shrinker.delegatec
Calls another shrinker class, passing through the relevant context
        variables.
        Note we explicitly do not pass through full.
        r

DEBUG)
print)
argsr
Shrinker.debugc
Shrink the value ``initial`` subject to the constraint that it
        satisfies ``predicate``.
        Returns the shrunk value.
        )
runr
clsr
shrinkerr
Shrinker.shrinkc
Run for an appropriate number of steps to improve the current value.
        If self.full is True, will run until no further improvements can
        be found.
        N
COMPLETE)
short_circuitr
run_stepr
prevr
Shrinker.runc
zcTry using ``value`` as a possible candidate improvement.
        Return True if it works.
        z	Rejected z
 as worse than self.current=Fz
shrinking to 
T)	r
check_invariants
left_is_betterr
addr
valuer
incorporateh
Shrinker.incorporatec
zdReturns True if make_immutable(value) == self.current after calling
        self.incorporate(value).T)
Shrinker.considerc
Convert value into an immutable (and hashable) representation of
        itself.
        It is these immutable versions that the shrinker will work on.
        Defaults to just returning the value.
        r
Shrinker.make_immutablec
Make appropriate assertions about the value to ensure that it is
        valid for this shrinker.
        Does nothing by default.
        N
NotImplementedErrorr6
Shrinker.check_invariantsc
Possibly attempt to do some shrinking.
        If this returns True, the ``run`` method will terminate early
        without doing any more work.
        Nr9
Shrinker.short_circuitc
zpReturns True if the left is strictly simpler than the right
        according to the standards of this shrinker.Nr9
left
rightr
Shrinker.left_is_betterc
z[Run a single step of the main shrink loop, attempting to improve the
        current value.Nr9
Shrinker.run_step)
FFN)
